title: Interpret Overall System API Page Status And Available System-Level Functions
summary: Use the Overall System Aveva API page to identify the current System State and interpret the source-described meaning of the listed system-level functions shown on the left side of the page: System Startup, System Bag Out, System Shutdown, and System Purge.
when_to_use: Use when viewing the Overall System Aveva API page and needing to understand the current system state display and the documented meaning of the system-level functions shown on the left side of the page.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to execute startup, bag out, shutdown, or purge actions beyond interpreting their documented purpose.
Do not use this runbook to infer undocumented behavior, corrective actions, or control logic not stated in the source.
Do not use this runbook if the API page is not accessible or the expected fields and items are not visible.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: View the Overall System Aveva API page, identify the displayed System State, and interpret the listed system-level functions using the source-provided descriptions.

safety_notes: This is a reference/interpretation procedure derived from training content and does not authorize control actions.
Do not invent additional actions or operational meaning beyond the source-provided descriptions.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Overall System Aveva API page
Visual access to the left-side system item list
Training source or documented descriptions for the API page items

access_required: Access to the Overall System Aveva API page
system_preconditions: The Overall System Aveva API page is available and the left-side system items are visible.
external_references: UPSFORTNA OptiSweep Training Day 1 training segment covering the Overall System Aveva API page

visual_references.description: Training slide showing the Overall System Aveva API page with System State, System Startup, System Bag Out, System Shutdown, and System Purge.

steps.title: Open or view the Overall System API page
steps.purpose: Establish the correct screen for interpreting system state and available system-level functions.
steps.instruction: Open or view the Overall System Aveva API page referred to in the training as the API page. Confirm the page shown is the Overall System page with the left-side list of system items.
steps.expected_result: The Overall System Aveva API page is visible.
steps.healthy_condition: The page displays the Overall System API view and the left-side system items are visible.
steps.failure_condition: The API page is not accessible, or the expected Overall System view and left-side items are not visible.

actions.label: View Overall System API page
actions.exact_action: Open or view the Overall System Aveva API page referred to in the training as the API page.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall System Aveva API page and the left-side list of system items.

stop_or_escalate_if: The API page is not accessible.
The expected Overall System page or left-side system items are not visible.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The speaker says all of this is through APIs and refers to the screen as the API page; the slide shows the Overall System Aveva API page with system-level items on the left.
Artifact shows the overall system Aveva API page with System State, System Startup, System Bag Out, System Shutdown, and System Purge.

steps.title: Read the System State field
steps.purpose: Identify the current state displayed for the system.
steps.instruction: Locate the System State field on the page and read the current state shown.
steps.expected_result: The current system state is identified from the System State field.
steps.healthy_condition: A readable System State value is displayed on the page.
steps.failure_condition: The System State field is missing, unreadable, or does not show a current state.

actions.label: Inspect System State
actions.exact_action: Locate the System State field on the page and read the current state shown.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: System State field on the Overall System Aveva API page.

stop_or_escalate_if: The System State field is not visible.
The displayed state cannot be read or interpreted from the page.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Slide text: 'System State ... Shows the current state the system is in.'

steps.title: Identify the listed system-level items
steps.purpose: Confirm which system-level functions are presented on the page for interpretation.
steps.instruction: Identify the listed system-level items on the left side of the page: System Startup, System Bag Out, System Shutdown, and System Purge.
steps.expected_result: The four listed system-level items are identified on the page.
steps.healthy_condition: System Startup, System Bag Out, System Shutdown, and System Purge are visible in the left-side list.
steps.failure_condition: One or more expected system-level items are not visible on the page.

actions.label: Inspect left-side system items
actions.exact_action: Identify the listed system-level items on the left side of the page: System Startup, System Bag Out, System Shutdown, and System Purge.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Left-side list showing System Startup, System Bag Out, System Shutdown, and System Purge.

stop_or_escalate_if: One or more expected system-level items are missing from view.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The slide shows the Overall System Aveva API page with system-level items on the left.
Artifact shows System State, System Startup, System Bag Out, System Shutdown, and System Purge.

steps.title: Interpret each listed function using the source descriptions
steps.purpose: Apply the training-provided meaning to each listed system-level function without extending beyond the source.
steps.instruction: Use the source-provided descriptions to interpret each item: System Startup stages AGVs for exchanges; System Bag Out or close out closes all chutes and exchanges totes with items; System Shutdown puts the system into a shutdown state for AGVs to rotate on the charger; and System Purge exchanges all totes and does not print a label at the tipper.
steps.expected_result: Each listed function is interpreted according to the source-provided description.
steps.healthy_condition: The meanings used match the training source descriptions for startup, bag out/close out, shutdown, and purge.
steps.failure_condition: The function meanings cannot be matched to the source, or additional unsupported meaning is being inferred.

actions.label: Interpret System Startup
actions.exact_action: Interpret System Startup as triggering OptiSweep to stage the AGVs for exchanges.

actions.label: Interpret System Bag Out
actions.exact_action: Interpret System Bag Out or close out as closing all chutes and exchanging totes with items.

actions.label: Interpret System Shutdown
actions.exact_action: Interpret System Shutdown as putting the system into a shutdown state for AGVs to rotate on the charger.

actions.label: Interpret System Purge
actions.exact_action: Interpret System Purge as exchanging all totes and not printing a label at the tipper.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The listed functions on the left side of the Overall System API page and their associated training descriptions.

stop_or_escalate_if: You are being asked to infer behavior or corrective actions not stated in the source.
The displayed function names do not match the source-described items.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Slide text: 'AGVs are kept in a shut down state between sorts to allow them to charge. System startup triggers OptiSweep to stage the AGVs for exchanges.'
Slide text says close out is used at the end of a sort to close all chutes and exchange all totes with items; transcript adds that close out only affects totes with items and prints labels.
Slide text: 'When the sort is done the system should be put in a shutdown state for AGVs to rotate on the charger.'
Transcript says purge is like close out but purges everything and does not print a label; slide text says 'Exchanges all totes and does not print a label at the tipper.'

steps.title: Record or communicate the displayed state and function meaning
steps.purpose: Capture or share the observed system state and the source-grounded interpretation of the relevant function.
steps.instruction: Record or communicate the displayed system state and the documented meaning of the relevant function without inventing additional actions beyond the source.
steps.expected_result: The current system state and relevant function meaning are communicated or documented using only source-supported descriptions.
steps.healthy_condition: The communicated or recorded information matches the displayed state and source-provided function descriptions.
steps.failure_condition: The information includes unsupported interpretation, undocumented actions, or assumptions beyond the source.

actions.label: Record or communicate interpretation
actions.exact_action: Record or communicate the displayed system state and the documented meaning of the relevant function without inventing additional actions beyond the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Displayed System State and the listed system-level functions being referenced for communication.

stop_or_escalate_if: You cannot access the page to verify the displayed state.
You would need to add undocumented behavior or corrective guidance to answer the request.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The source describes meanings of page items more clearly than it describes exact button-press actions, so the candidate focuses on interpretation rather than execution.

success_criteria: The Overall System Aveva API page is identified correctly.
The current System State is read from the page.
The listed system-level items are identified as System Startup, System Bag Out, System Shutdown, and System Purge.
The meaning of each listed function is interpreted using only the source-provided descriptions.
The displayed state and relevant function meaning are recorded or communicated without unsupported additions.
healthy_conditions: The Overall System API page is accessible and visible.
The System State field is present and readable.
The left-side system items are visible.
Interpretation remains limited to the source-provided descriptions.
failure_conditions: The API page is not accessible.
The expected fields or listed items are not visible.
The System State field cannot be read.
The user attempts to infer undocumented behavior or corrective actions beyond the source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the API page is not accessible or the expected fields and items are not visible.
Escalate if the displayed page content does not match the training-described Overall System API page.
Do not provide undocumented behavior or corrective actions; seek SME clarification if more detail is required.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall System Aveva API page with System State and left-side system-level functions.

missing_details: The source does not provide exact navigation steps to open the Overall System Aveva API page.
The source does not provide button-press execution steps for startup, bag out, shutdown, or purge in this segment.
The source does not provide a time estimate for this reference procedure.
The source does not specify supporting roles beyond the operator audience.
The source does not define exact escalation contacts or routing.
